title: Intraperitoneal Glucose Tolerance Test Protocol
author: Fawaz G. Haj
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yp4fvqw

steps.instruction: Fast mice for 16hours by taking away food the day before (3:00pm)
The following day, Calibrate the glucose meter according to the manufacturer’s instructions.
Deprive mice from water then remove approximately 5 μl of blood (one drop) from thetail via a tail tip cut and transfer directly onto a glucose indicator strip.
Measure blood glucose immediately in a glucometer
Give the mouse an intraperitoneal injection of Glucose (2g/kg) with a 27 G needle

Continue to take blood samples from the initial tail cut before the glucose injection and at 15, 30, 60 and 120 min.Between each of these time points, return the mouse to its cage and monitor it every minute.
NOTE: At the end of the experiment, wipe tail with 70% alcohol and allow drying. Ensure that blood loss from the tail stopped before placing the animal back to its cage.
